turns.assistantMessage: Improving dream recall can be achieved through consistent practice and a few strategies. Keeping a dream journal is an excellent start, but to enhance recall, try to keep the journal beside your bed and write down any fragments of dreams you remember as soon as you wake up. Even if it's just a feeling or a single image, noting it down can help. Establishing a consistent sleep schedule and creating a relaxing bedtime routine can also improve the vividness and recall of dreams. As for influencing what you dream about, techniques such as reality checking (checking your surroundings to see if you're dreaming) and maintaining a dream diary can increase your self-awareness, which may carry over into your dreams, potentially allowing you to recognize when you're dreaming (lucidity). However, directly influencing dream content is more challenging and may involve practices like meditation or Wake-Back-To-Bed techniques, where you set your alarm to wake up after a few hours, stay awake for a short time, and then go back to bed, which can increase the likelihood of entering a lucid dream state. Exploring these practices and being patient with yourself as you develop greater awareness and control over your dreams can provide valuable insights into your subconscious mind and its connection to your waking experiences, including the 'axis window'.
